
```{r}
# Load necessary packages
if (!require(readxl)) install.packages("readxl")
if (!require(ggplot2)) install.packages("ggplot2")
if (!require(GGally)) install.packages("GGally")
if (!require(ggridges)) install.packages("ggridges")
if (!require(dplyr)) install.packages("dplyr")
if (!require(forcats)) install.packages("forcats")

library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(GGally)
library(ggridges)
library(dplyr)
library(forcats)


```
```{r}
df <- read_excel("~/Desktop/Final/Bhakti_Final/P_final/train_data.xlsx")
glimpse(df)
```


```{r}
# Load necessary packages
packages <- c("readxl", "ggplot2", "dplyr", "ggridges", "GGally", "forcats", "plotly", "scales", "reshape2")
install_if_missing <- function(p) { if (!require(p, character.only = TRUE)) install.packages(p) }
invisible(lapply(packages, install_if_missing))
lapply(packages, library, character.only = TRUE)
```

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = Age, y = fct_rev(`Education level`), fill = `Education level`)) +
  geom_density_ridges(scale = 1.3, alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Age Distribution Across Education Levels", x = "Age", y = "Education Level")

```
```{r}
df %>%
  count(`Is high risk`) %>%
  mutate(percentage = n / sum(n)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = "", y = percentage, fill = as.factor(`Is high risk`))) +
  geom_col(width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(percentage)), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5)) +
  labs(title = "Credit Risk Distribution", fill = "Is High Risk") +
  theme_void()


```

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = as.factor(`Is high risk`), y = Income, fill = as.factor(`Is high risk`))) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, outlier.shape = NA) +
  labs(title = "Income Distribution by Risk Category", x = "Is High Risk", y = "Income") +
  theme_minimal()

```

```{r}
df %>%
  count(`Has a work phone`, `Has an email`) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(`Has a work phone`), y = n, fill = as.factor(`Has an email`))) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Work Phone vs Email Access", x = "Has Work Phone", y = "Count", fill = "Has Email") +
  theme_minimal()

```
```{r}
df %>%
  count(`Employment status`, `Marital status`) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = `Employment status`, y = n, fill = `Marital status`)) +
  geom_col(position = "fill") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Employment Type across Marital Status", y = "Proportion")

```
```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = as.factor(`Children count`), y = Income, fill = as.factor(`Children count`))) +
  geom_violin(trim = FALSE, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = "median", geom = "point", shape = 20, size = 3, color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Income Distribution by Number of Children", x = "Children Count", y = "Income") +
  theme(legend.position = "none")


```
```{r}
library(dplyr)

df %>%
  group_by(`Children count`) %>%
  summarise(mean_income = mean(Income, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_income = sd(Income, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = as.factor(`Children count`), y = mean_income)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_income - sd_income, ymax = mean_income + sd_income), width = 0.2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Average Income by Children Count (with SD Error Bars)", x = "Children", y = "Mean Income")

```
```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(x = `Education level`, fill = Gender)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge") +
  labs(title = "Education Level by Gender", x = "Education Level", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```
```{r}
df %>%
  group_by(`Education level`) %>%
  summarise(avg_age = mean(`Account age`, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  slice_max(avg_age, n = 10) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(`Education level`, avg_age), y = avg_age)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Top Education Levels by Avg Account Age", x = "Education Level", y = "Avg Account Age") +
  theme_minimal()



```

```{r}
library(tidyr)

df %>%
  group_by(Gender, `Marital status`) %>%
  summarise(avg_income = mean(Income, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Marital status`, values_from = avg_income)

```
```{r}
library(tidyr)

df %>%
  group_by(`Education level`, `Employment status`) %>%
  summarise(avg_income = mean(Income, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Employment status`, values_from = avg_income)

```
```{r}
library(tidyr)

df %>%
  group_by(`Has a car`, `Is high risk`) %>%
  summarise(avg_account_age = mean(`Account age`, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = `Is high risk`, values_from = avg_account_age,
              names_prefix = "Risk_")


```
